name: Chinese Barbecued Spareribs
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the spare ribs into two or three strips across the bone.
2. Rub the ribs all over with 1 tablespoon sugar and let stand for 30 minutes.
3. Preheat the oven to 450°F.
4. Blend the fermented red bean curd, light soy sauce, bean paste, hoisin sauce, and sesame paste together in a bowl until smooth.
5. Rub the mixture evenly over the rack of ribs.
6. Line a deep baking dish with aluminum foil and place a rack inside it. Arrange the seasoned ribs on the rack.
7. Put the pan in the oven and roast for 20 minutes.
8. Turn the ribs and roast for an additional 15 minutes.
9. Meanwhile, heat about 1/4 cup honey with 2 tablespoons dark soy sauce in a small saucepan over low heat until combined.
10. Baste one side of the ribs with the honey mixture.
11. Roast the basted ribs for 10 more minutes.
12. Turn the ribs and baste again with the remaining honey mixture.
13. Continue roasting for an additional 5 to 10 minutes, or until the ribs are tender and caramelized.
14. Remove the ribs from the oven and let them sit for 10 minutes before cutting into separate pieces.
15. Serve hot.
